independentBranchPoints.note: Database searches are fully independent — assign one sub-agent per source. They converge at p1_merge for dedupe.
Each extraction agent reads ALL included papers but extracts ONE dimension. Independent and parallelizable.
Synthesis themes are independent. Each agent consumes the master evidence table.
Manuscript sections can be drafted in parallel. Final assembly happens at p5_assemble.
Review angles are independent. Compile feedback at p6_compile before revision loop.

nodes.label: Kick-off
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Initiate the greenwashing literature review.

Scope: cross-disciplinary (marketing, management, sustainability, finance, law). Author: the user. Output target: a synthesis review article suitable for a peer-reviewed journal (target outlet TBD).

nodes.label: Define RQs
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Research Question Definition Agent.

Decide 1–3 focal RQs and their rationale. Candidate RQs:
- How has the greenwashing concept been defined and operationalized across disciplines?
- What are the antecedents, mechanisms, and consequences of greenwashing?
- Which methodological approaches dominate and what are the gaps?

Output: 1–3 RQs, each with scope statement and exclusion notes.

nodes.label: Inclusion criteria
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Inclusion / Exclusion Criteria Agent.

Specify: time window (e.g., 1990–present); language (English unless multilingual review); peer-reviewed only or include grey/working papers; disciplines; study types (empirical only, or include theoretical/conceptual); unit of analysis (firm, claim, product, fund).

Output: criteria document, versioned.

nodes.label: Search strategy
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Search Strategy Agent.

Build the search-string library: keyword sets ('greenwashing' OR 'green-washing' OR 'green washing' OR 'greenwash*'); related terms (sustainability claim*, eco-label*, ESG misrepresentation, environmental disclosure); Boolean operators; field limits; per-database syntax (Scopus TITLE-ABS-KEY, WoS TS=, etc.).

Pilot the strings on 1–2 databases first; adjust precision/recall.

Output: search-string spec table.

nodes.label: Protocol doc
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Protocol Documentation Agent.

Even if not formally registered (PROSPERO, OSF), document the full protocol: RQs, criteria, search strategy, screening rules, extraction template, synthesis approach, IRR plan.

Freeze the protocol BEFORE starting searches — changes after this point should be tracked as deviations.

Output: protocol.md (versioned, in the project repo).

nodes.label: P1 · Search fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Run searches in parallel across 7 sources. Each is independent and can be delegated to its own sub-agent.

All branches converge at p1_merge for deduplication.

nodes.label: Web of Science
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Web of Science Search Agent.

Apply the search strings from p0_strategy. Document: query string, date searched, hit count, filters applied. Export records (RIS or BibTeX).

Output: wos_records.ris + wos_query.log.

nodes.label: Scopus
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Scopus Search Agent.

Same protocol as Web of Science, with Scopus-native syntax. Often the broadest single source for management/marketing.

Output: scopus_records.ris + scopus_query.log.

nodes.label: Google Scholar
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Google Scholar Search Agent.

Use Publish-or-Perish or scholarly to extract ≤ 1000 records. Caveat: low precision, high recall — expect heavier deduplication.

Output: gs_records.ris + gs_query.log.

nodes.label: EBSCO Business
nodes.purposeInstructions:
EBSCO Business Source Complete Search Agent.

Particularly relevant for marketing and management greenwashing literature; captures sources WoS / Scopus may miss.

Output: ebsco_records.ris + ebsco_query.log.

nodes.label: JSTOR
nodes.purposeInstructions:
JSTOR Search Agent.

Useful for older/foundational papers and humanities/policy framings of greenwashing.

Output: jstor_records.ris + jstor_query.log.

nodes.label: SSRN · preprints
nodes.purposeInstructions:
SSRN / Preprint Search Agent.

Captures working papers and recent finance/ESG greenwashing work that hasn't yet appeared in journals. Also check OSF, SocArXiv, ResearchGate (with caution).

Output: ssrn_records.ris + preprint sources log.

nodes.label: Citation tracing
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Citation Tracking Agent.

Seed papers (must include): Lyon & Maxwell (2011, JEMS); Delmas & Burbano (2011, CMR); TerraChoice 'Seven Sins'; Bowen (2014); Lyon & Montgomery (2015 review). Also recent reviews.

Forward tracing: who cites these seeds? Backward tracing: what do these seeds cite?

Output: citation-traced records, flagged with seed source.

nodes.label: Merge sources
nodes.purposeInstructions: Source Merge Agent. Combine all 7 source streams into one library. No filtering yet — just union with provenance tags.

nodes.label: Deduplicate
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Deduplication Agent.

Dedupe by DOI first; then by title+year fuzzy match (Levenshtein ≤ 3 on normalized title). Resolve conflicts: prefer published over preprint; prefer journal version over conference; keep the version with more metadata.

Output: deduplicated_corpus.ris + dedup_log.csv.

nodes.label: Import to manager
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Reference Manager Import Agent.

Load deduplicated corpus into Zotero / Rayyan / Covidence. Set up tag taxonomy (include / exclude / maybe; reason codes; theme tags).

Output: project library ready for screening.

nodes.label: TA screen (loop)
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Title-Abstract Screening Agent (iterates over every record).

Apply inclusion/exclusion criteria from p0_criteria using ONLY title and abstract. Mark each record include / exclude / maybe with a reason code.

If using AI-assisted screening (e.g., Rayyan ranking), still hand-review borderline cases.
nodes.loopDefinition: For each deduplicated record: read title + abstract → apply criteria → assign label + reason code → record decision.

stopConditions.text: All deduplicated records have a TA-screening decision

nodes.label: Full-text retrieve
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Full-Text Retrieval Agent.

For TA-passing records (include + maybe), retrieve full PDFs. Use library subscriptions, ILL, author requests, ResearchGate as fallback.

Flag unavailable items — if material to the corpus, escalate.

Output: full-text PDFs + retrieval log + 'unavailable' list.

nodes.label: FT screen (loop)
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Full-Text Screening Agent (iterates over retrieved full texts).

Apply criteria to full text. Mark final include / exclude with detailed reason. Only the final 'include' set proceeds to extraction.
nodes.loopDefinition: For each retrieved full text: skim methods + key sections → verify criteria match → final include/exclude with reason.

stopConditions.text: All retrieved full texts have a final-screening decision

nodes.label: IRR check
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Inter-Rater Reliability Agent.

If multi-coder: sample 10–20% double-screened; compute Cohen's kappa or % agreement. Discuss disagreements; recalibrate criteria; re-screen if needed.

If solo coder: document the absence of IRR transparently in the methods section as a limitation.

Output: IRR report (or solo-coder methodological note).

nodes.label: Final corpus
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Final Corpus Agent.

Lock the included papers list. Build a PRISMA-style flow diagram: records identified → deduplicated → TA-screened → FT-retrieved → FT-screened → included.

Output: corpus.csv + prisma_flow.png.

nodes.label: P3 · Extraction fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Extraction fan-out: 6 dimensions extracted in parallel.

Each extraction agent reads EVERY included paper but extracts only ONE dimension. This is fully parallelizable across sub-agents.

nodes.label: Bibliometric
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Bibliometric Extraction Agent.

Per paper, extract: year, journal, journal discipline, authors, author affiliations, country/region of authors, country/region of data (if empirical), citation count (Scopus + GS), study type tag.

Output: biblio_table.csv.

nodes.label: Definitions
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Definition Extraction Agent.

Per paper, extract:
- Verbatim definition(s) of greenwashing.
- Cited definitional source(s) (Lyon & Maxwell, Delmas & Burbano, TerraChoice, Bowen, Walker & Wan, etc.).
- Type of greenwashing addressed: firm-level / claim-level / product-level / executional / selective disclosure / decoupling / greenhushing.
- Whether definition is original to the paper or imported.

Output: definition_table.csv.

nodes.label: Theory
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Theory Extraction Agent.

Per paper, identify the theoretical lens(es) used: signaling theory, legitimacy theory, institutional theory, stakeholder theory, agency theory, information asymmetry, attribution theory, persuasion knowledge, etc.

Note when paper is atheoretical or theory is implicit. Note theory combinations.

Output: theory_table.csv.

nodes.label: Methods
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Methods Extraction Agent.

Per paper, extract: research design (experiment, archival, survey, content analysis, ethnography, mixed-methods, conceptual); sample (size, type, context); data sources; analytic approach (regression, SEM, DiD, qualitative coding, etc.); validity claims and threats addressed.

Output: methods_table.csv.

nodes.label: Findings
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Findings Extraction Agent.

Per paper, extract key findings as discrete claims. For each claim: direction, magnitude (effect size if reported), context, moderators noted, p-value or CI.

Flag null findings, unexpected directions, contradictions across studies.

Output: findings_table.csv (long format — one row per claim).

nodes.label: Constructs
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Constructs & Measurement Extraction Agent.

Per paper, extract: constructs measured (greenwashing perception, skepticism, brand trust, purchase intent, financial performance, stock returns, etc.); how each is operationalized (validated scale, content-analysis dictionary, archival proxy); reliability/validity reported.

Output: constructs_table.csv.

nodes.label: Extraction merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge all 6 extraction tables on paper-ID. Validate completeness — every included paper appears in every dimension. Flag missing cells for follow-up.

nodes.label: Coding QA
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Coding QA Agent.

Double-code 10% of papers (random sample). Compute % agreement / kappa per dimension. Resolve coding-frame issues; update extraction dictionaries if recurring confusion.

Output: coding_qa_report.md.

nodes.label: Evidence table
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Master Evidence Table Agent.

Wide-format table joining all extraction dimensions on paper-ID. This is the single source of truth for synthesis.

Output: evidence_table.xlsx (one sheet per dimension + a wide master sheet).

nodes.label: P4 · Synthesis fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions: Synthesize across 6 themes in parallel. Each synthesis agent reads the master evidence table from its angle.

nodes.label: Synth: definitions
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Definitional Synthesis Agent.

Map the typology of greenwashing definitions across the corpus. Identify clusters (e.g., communication-based vs performance-based; intentional vs unintentional; firm-level vs claim-level). Trace evolution over time. Note contemporary extensions: greenhushing, ESG/fund greenwashing, AI-generated greenwash.

Output: definitional_synthesis.md (3–4 pages) + typology figure draft.

nodes.label: Synth: theory
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Theoretical Synthesis Agent.

Identify dominant theoretical lenses. Note over- vs under-used theories. Surface integrative frameworks (e.g., Delmas & Burbano's greenwashing drivers framework). Suggest theoretical extensions or imports from adjacent literatures.

Output: theoretical_synthesis.md.

nodes.label: Synth: methods
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Methodological Synthesis Agent.

Inventory methods used (% experimental, % archival, % content analysis, etc.). Note dominant samples and contexts (e.g., consumer experiments in WEIRD populations; archival US-listed firms).

Identify methodological gaps: longitudinal studies, non-Western samples, qualitative depth, causal designs, replication.

Output: methods_synthesis.md.

nodes.label: Synth: antecedents
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Antecedents Synthesis Agent.

What drives greenwashing? Aggregate evidence at:
- Firm level (size, industry, prior performance, ownership, leadership)
- Institutional level (regulation, scrutiny, certification regimes)
- Individual level (manager incentives, narcissism, ethical climate)
- Market level (consumer demand, competitor behavior)

Output: antecedents_map.md + figure draft.

nodes.label: Synth: consequences
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Consequences Synthesis Agent.

Outcomes of greenwashing across stakeholders:
- Consumers (skepticism, trust, WTP, switching)
- Firms (reputation, financial performance, stock returns, litigation)
- Markets (industry-level spillovers, race-to-the-bottom)
- Regulators / society (regulatory action, public trust in sustainability claims)

Output: consequences_map.md + figure draft.

nodes.label: Synth: gaps
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Gaps & Future Research Agent.

Identify what the literature has NOT addressed: under-studied contexts (Global South, SMEs, B2B), theoretical lacunae, methodological weaknesses, emerging phenomena (greenhushing, AI-generated greenwash, ESG-fund greenwashing, finance-side disclosure misrepresentation, supply-chain claims).

Output: gap_list.md (each gap stated as: 'literature has X, but lacks Y, which matters because Z').

nodes.label: Synth merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Converge synthesis themes. Cross-check for contradictions across themes (e.g., does the theory synthesis align with the antecedents synthesis?).

nodes.label: Meta-narrative
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Meta-Narrative Agent.

Build the overarching arc the manuscript will tell:
'Greenwashing literature has accomplished X across Y disciplines, but is missing Z; this review contributes by W.'

Output: meta_narrative.md + tentative thesis statement (1–2 sentences).

nodes.label: Advisor review
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Advisor / Co-Author Review Gate (human-in-the-loop).

Share the master evidence table + 6 synthesis docs + meta-narrative with PI / co-authors. Hold for substantive feedback before drafting.

Capture revisions to syntheses or meta-narrative; loop back to p4 if major rework needed (manual).

nodes.label: P5 · Drafting fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions: Draft 6 manuscript sections in parallel. Each section pulls from synthesis outputs.

nodes.label: Intro
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Introduction Drafter.

Structure: hook → why greenwashing matters now (regulatory, market, ESG context) → what's known → what's missing → our contribution → roadmap.

Cite seminal papers (Lyon & Maxwell 2011; Delmas & Burbano 2011; Bowen 2014; recent reviews). Avoid throat-clearing.

Output: intro_draft.md (~1500 words).

nodes.label: Methods
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Review-Methods Drafter.

Document the review protocol per PRISMA-style standards (or transparently note deviations): search strategy, databases, dates, inclusion/exclusion, screening process, IRR, extraction template, synthesis approach.

Include PRISMA flow diagram.

Output: methods_section.md.

nodes.label: Results: defs
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Results — Definitions Drafter.

Translate the definitional synthesis into prose. Use a typology figure or table. Surface tensions across definitions and their consequences for downstream measurement.

Output: results_definitions.md.

nodes.label: Results: theory
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Results — Theories Drafter.

Narrate the theoretical landscape. Show which theories appear where and why. Highlight under-used lenses (e.g., persuasion knowledge, attribution theory, dynamic capabilities).

Output: results_theory.md.

nodes.label: Results: findings
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Results — Findings Drafter.

Synthesize the antecedents + consequences narratives with key empirical illustrations. Use a 2x2 or radial figure to organize.

Do not list studies one by one; organize by claim and let evidence accrete around each claim.

Output: results_findings.md.

nodes.label: Discussion
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Discussion Drafter.

Structure: so-what for theory → so-what for practice/policy → future research agenda (drawn from p4_gaps) → limitations of the review itself → conclusion.

Output: discussion_section.md.

nodes.label: Sections merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Combine drafted sections. Cross-check that intro promises map to discussion delivery.

nodes.label: Assemble v0.1
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Manuscript Assembler.

Stitch sections into a single document: front matter, abstract (write last), keywords, sections, references, figures, tables, appendices.

Run a basic readability/length check vs target outlet word limits.

Output: manuscript_v0.1.docx + figures/ directory.

nodes.label: P6 · Review fan-out
nodes.purposeInstructions: Review the manuscript from 5 angles in parallel. Each reviewer is an independent agent.

nodes.label: Methods review
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Methodological Reviewer.

Did we follow systematic-review standards (or transparently document deviations)? PRISMA flow correct? Search reproducible? Screening and extraction documented? Sample size sufficient for synthesis claims?

Output: method_review_notes.md with severity-tagged issues.

nodes.label: Theory review
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Theoretical Contribution Reviewer.

Is the theoretical contribution clearly stated, substantiated by evidence, and non-obvious? Does the meta-narrative deliver on the promised gap? Is the future-research agenda actionable?

Output: theory_review_notes.md.

nodes.label: Writing review
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Writing Reviewer.

Clarity, prose quality, transitions, jargon load, audience fit. Section-by-section line edits. Flag passive-voice abuse, hedging, and run-on sentences.

Output: tracked-changes file (manuscript_v0.1_writing.docx).

nodes.label: Citation review
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Citation Reviewer.

Verify: every claim has a citation; every citation matches its claim (no misattribution); reference list is complete and consistently formatted; DOIs validate; no broken citations.

IMPORTANT: pay special attention to definitional attribution — greenwashing has many similar-but-distinct definitions, and misattribution is common.

Output: citation_review_report.md + corrected references.bib.

nodes.label: Originality check
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Originality Reviewer.

Run plagiarism check (Turnitin / iThenticate). Run AI-detection per submission requirements. Verify no inadvertent self-plagiarism with prior author work.

IMPORTANT: if any text passed through AI tools, strip markdown and verify natural prose before submitting to detectors.

Output: originality_report.md + flagged passages.

nodes.label: Review merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Merge all 5 review streams into one feedback set.

nodes.label: Feedback compile
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Feedback Compiler.

Aggregate all reviewer notes. Prioritize: must-fix vs should-fix vs nice-to-have. Group by section and assign to revision tasks.

Output: revision_priority_list.md.

nodes.label: Revision (loop)
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Revision Loop.

Iterate revision rounds: address feedback batch → re-draft affected sections → light self-review → update revision log.

Do not aim for perfection in one pass — each iteration absorbs the highest-priority feedback first. Stop when must-fixes are resolved or revision budget exhausted.
nodes.loopDefinition: For each revision round: pick top-priority feedback batch (per p6_compile) → address by editing sections → light self-critique → commit version (manuscript_v0.X.docx).

stopConditions.text: All must-fix items resolved AND co-author/PI green-light

nodes.label: Final proof
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Final Proofread Agent.

Full-manuscript pass: typos, grammar, citation matches, figure/table labels and captions, cross-references, consistency of terminology.

Output: clean manuscript ready for formatting.

nodes.label: Format for outlet
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Outlet Formatting Agent.

Apply target journal's style (e.g., AMJ, JOM, JBE, Org & Env, JCR, Sustainability, JCP). Word count, reference style, structured abstract if required, figure/table format, supplementary materials.

Output: outlet-formatted manuscript + cover letter draft.

nodes.label: Outlet path?
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Branch decision: deposit pre-print first, or go straight to journal?

Output 1 (top): preprint path (recommended for visibility, especially if outlet allows it).
Output 2 (bottom): direct-to-journal path (for outlets that disallow preprints, or when blind review confidentiality is preferred).

nodes.label: Preprint deposit
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Preprint Deposit Agent.

Post to SSRN / OSF / SocArXiv. Acquire DOI. Update CV / lab page / social with citation.

Output: preprint URL + DOI.

nodes.label: Journal submit
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Journal Submission Agent.

Submit through editorial system. Confirm submission. Track manuscript ID. Editorial decision is handled outside this workflow — expect a separate revision pipeline upon R&R.

Output: submission confirmation + manuscript ID.

nodes.label: Submit merge
nodes.purposeInstructions: Both submission paths converge. The cycle is complete from a workflow perspective.

nodes.label: Submitted
nodes.purposeInstructions:
Submission complete.

Next: track for editorial decision; archive all artifacts (protocol, search logs, screening logs, evidence table, syntheses, drafts, review notes, revision logs) in a versioned project folder for reproducibility and future R&R rounds.
